solution.p: We will factor 2r^{2}-9r+10 where a=2, b=-9, and c=10.
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (2) \cdot (10) = 20.

The number 20 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(20)\amp -1(-20)\amp 2(10)\amp -2(-10)
ewline4(5)\amp -4(-5)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -9, and this sum happens with (-4)+(-5)=-9 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]2r^{2}-9r+10\amp =2r^{2}-4r-5r+10
ewline \amp =2r\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)-5\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)
ewline \amp =\left(2r-5ight)\mathopen{}\left(r-2ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 2r^{2}-9r+10 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 2, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(2)\amp \end{array}
The third term, 10, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(10)\amp -1(-10)\amp 2(5)\amp -2(-5)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 2r^{2}-9r+10 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]2r^{2}-9r+10\amp =\left(2r-5\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-2\right)\end{aligned}
